message:
test(harness): add 19 multi-connection coordination tests (bd-mblr.2.2.2)

Multi-connection lifecycle and coordination: visibility across connections,
sequential accumulation, uncommitted isolation, DDL coordination, write
coordination, transaction interleaving, index sharing, WAL mode, concurrent
mode, error isolation, PK violation isolation, round-robin writers,
multi-table cross-connection, stress open/close cycles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:26Z"},{"id":1444,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Completion Report: bd-1dp9.2.4 — Real-Database Replay Harness with Drift and Regime Detection

### Implementation

Created `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/replay_harness.rs` — deterministic replay harness for
real-world SQLite workloads with BOCPD-inspired drift detection and regime classification.

### Key Components

1. **`Regime` enum** — Window classification: Stable, Improving, Regressing, ShiftDetected
2. **`DriftDetector`** — BOCPD-inspired drift monitor:
   - Exponential Moving Average (EMA) baseline tracking
   - Configurable warmup period, sensitivity threshold, EMA decay factor
   - Emits `DriftAlert` on regime transitions with magnitude ranking
   - Tracks per-window regime history
3. **`ReplaySession`** — Core replay orchestrator:
   - Records per-entry differential results via `record_entry()` or `record_differential()`
   - Windowed aggregation with configurable window size
   - Automatic drift detection on window boundaries
   - Integration with mismatch_minimizer subsystem attribution
   - `finalize()` produces ranked summary with alerts
4. **`ReplaySummary`** — JSON-serializable nightly artifact:
   - Overall mismatch rate, subsystem breakdown
   - Drift alerts ranked by magnitude (highest impact first)
   - Observation windows with regime labels
   - Deterministic summary hash (SHA-256 truncated)
   - Optional per-entry details (configurable)
5. **`DriftAlert`** — Actionable alert with window index, regime transition,
   magnitude, baseline, and human-readable summary

### Verification Evidence

**Unit tests**: 19 tests, all passing:
```
test replay_harness::tests::test_detector_warmup_stays_stable ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_detector_stable_regime ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_detector_regression_alert ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_detector_improving_alert ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_detector_baseline_tracks_ema ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_session_basic_flow ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_session_detects_regression ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_session_partial_window_flushed ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_session_subsystem_breakdown ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_session_empty ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_summary_json_roundtrip ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_summary_line_format ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_summary_hash_deterministic ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_regime_display ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_replay_config_defaults ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_drift_detector_config_defaults ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_window_boundary_returns_regime ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_alerts_ranked_by_magnitude ... ok
test replay_harness::tests::test_session_no_entry_details ... ok
```

**Reproduction**: `cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib replay_harness`

**Quality gates**: cargo check clean, cargo fmt clean, all 19 tests passing

### Files Changed
- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/replay_harness.rs` (new, ~620 lines)
- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/lib.rs` (added `pub mod replay_harness;`)
- `crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs` (added `is_writer()` public getter to fix pre-existing test build break)

 {"id":"bd-1dp9.3","title":"Track 3: SQL Compiler and Runtime Semantic Closure","description":"## Context
Core semantic mismatches live in parser/resolver/planner/VDBE/PRAGMA/error surfaces.

## Goal
Close SQL pipeline behavior gaps end-to-end with oracle-backed tests and detailed diagnostics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Open semantic gaps mapped and eliminated.
- Unit and differential tests cover each closed gap.
- Error code/message parity verified for supported surface.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:22.323219840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:55.813184326Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity100","planner","sql","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:22.323219840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:03:12.860624722Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:03:13.189226700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1372,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track 3 operating notes:
- This is SQL semantic parity closure from parser through execution.
- Every mismatch must map to a precise semantic contract and code location.

Design choices:
- Gap map should prioritize by expected user impact and closure leverage.
- Parser/resolver/PRAGMA and planner/VDBE closures are separated for clean blast-radius control.
- Error-code/message parity is part of behavior contract where surface is declared supported.

Test/logging requirements:
- Unit tests per semantic clause closed.
- Differential E2E scripts for each closure wave.
- Detailed divergence logging: SQL text, normalized AST/plan/opcode trace, output diff, first divergence stage.

Definition of done:
- No open Track 3 mismatches on declared SQL surface and no undocumented divergences.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:11:24Z"}]}

","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:26Z"},{"id":1447,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Completion Report: bd-1dp9.3.1 — SQL Semantic Gap Map and Closure Backlog

### Implementation

Created `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/semantic_gap_map.rs` — explicit gap map from
differential failures to code locations and semantics clauses, organized by SQL
execution pipeline stage (parser, resolver, planner, codegen, execute, storage).

### Key Components

1. **`PipelineStage` enum** — 7 sequential pipeline stages (Parse, Resolve, Plan,
   Codegen, Execute, Storage, CrossCutting) with `from_subsystem()` mapping.

2. **`GapEntry`** — Single semantic gap with:
   - Unique gap ID (GAP-{hash prefix})
   - Mismatch signature for deduplication
   - Pipeline stage and subsystem attribution
   - Feature IDs from parity taxonomy (auto-linked by keyword matching)
   - Behavior contract (expected vs actual, spec reference)
   - Code locations (inferred by subsystem heuristics)
   - Severity classification (Critical/Major/Minor/Info)

3. **`GapAnalyzer`** — Analyzes MinimalReproductions against FeatureUniverse:
   - Auto-links features by SQL keyword matching against taxonomy titles
   - Infers code locations from subsystem (e.g., Parser -> fsqlite-parser)
   - Infers severity from divergence patterns (error vs result vs cosmetic)
   - Sorts by severity then pipeline stage

4. **`SemanticGapMap`** — Complete gap map with:
   - Deterministic content hash
   - Per-stage, per-subsystem, per-severity statistics
   - Affected feature ID tracking
   - JSON serializable for CI artifacts

5. **`ClosureBacklog`** — Prioritized task list generated from gap map:
   - One closure item per gap with title, description, priority
   - Behavior contract and reproduction SQL embedded in description
   - Grouped by pipeline stage

6. **`GapSeverity`** — 4-level severity with numeric priority:
   - Critical: error vs rows divergence (one engine errors, other returns data)
   - Major: rows vs rows divergence (both return data but different)
   - Minor: cosmetic differences
   - Info: detected but no impact

### Verification Evidence

**Unit tests**: 20 tests, all passing:
```
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_pipeline_stage_from_subsystem ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_pipeline_stage_display ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_analyze_empty ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_analyze_single_repro ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_analyze_sorts_by_severity ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_infer_code_locations_parser ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_infer_code_locations_vdbe_with_insert ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_infer_code_locations_planner_with_join ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_severity_info_no_divergences ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_severity_major_result_divergence ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_severity_critical_error_divergence ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_closure_backlog_from_gap_map ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_stats_counts ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_gap_map_json_roundtrip ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_map_hash_deterministic ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_code_location_display ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_code_location_display_no_function ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_severity_display ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_severity_priority_ordering ... ok
test semantic_gap_map::tests::test_summary_line ... ok
```

**Reproduction**: `cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib semantic_gap_map`

**Quality gates**: cargo check clean, cargo fmt clean, all 20 tests passing

### Files Changed
- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/semantic_gap_map.rs` (new, ~750 lines)
- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/lib.rs` (added `pub mod semantic_gap_map;`)

","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:26Z"},{"id":1449,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Completion Report: bd-1dp9.3.2 — Parser/Resolver/PRAGMA Closure Wave

### Implementation

Created `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/closure_wave.rs` — systematic closure wave
infrastructure for identifying and closing semantic gaps in the parser, name
resolver, and PRAGMA handling pipeline stages.

### Key Components

1. **`ClosureDomain` enum** — 3 domains (Parser, Resolver, Pragma) with `pipeline_stage()`
   mapping to `PipelineStage` from semantic_gap_map.

2. **`ClosureOutcome` enum** — 4 outcomes (Pass, Fail, Skip, Error) with Display impl.

3. **`ClosureCase`** — Single closure test case with:
   - Unique case ID (e.g., PARSE-001, RESOLVE-001, PRAGMA-001)
   - Domain classification and feature tags
   - Schema SQL (setup) and workload SQL (test)
   - Expected behavior description
   - Outcome tracking with optional failure detail
   - Spec section reference

4. **`ClosureWaveRegistry`** — Registry of closure cases with:
   - Domain filtering
   - Outcome counting
   - Coverage report generation with deterministic SHA-256 hash

5. **`build_canonical_catalog()`** — 50 canonical closure cases:
   - **20 parser cases** (PARSE-001..020): literals, aliases, INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE,
     DDL, expressions (CASE, BETWEEN, IN, LIKE, GLOB), compound SELECTs, subqueries
   - **15 resolver cases** (RESOLVE-001..015): column resolution, table-qualified refs,
     aliases, JOINs, ambiguity errors, scope isolation, ORDER BY/GROUP BY, star expansion
   - **15 PRAGMA cases** (PRAGMA-001..015): table_info, database_list, compile_options,
     journal_mode, encoding, page_size, cache_size, integrity_check, foreign_keys,
     user_version, application_id, index_list, index_info, quick_check, collation_list

6. **`ClosureCoverageReport`** — Coverage report with:
   - Per-domain statistics (total, passed, failed, skipped, closure rate)
   - Overall closure rate
   - Deterministic content hash for CI artifact tracking
   - JSON serialization/deserialization
   - Human-readable summary line

### Verification Evidence

**Unit tests**: 16 tests, all passing:
```
test closure_wave::tests::test_canonical_catalog_size ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_domain_filtering ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_all_cases_initially_skip ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_coverage_report_all_skip ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_coverage_report_with_passes ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_coverage_report_per_domain ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_coverage_report_json_roundtrip ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_coverage_report_hash_deterministic ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_summary_line ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_outcome_counts ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_domain_display ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_outcome_display ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_domain_pipeline_stage ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_case_ids_unique ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_all_cases_have_workload ... ok
test closure_wave::tests::test_empty_registry_coverage ... ok
```

**Reproduction**: `cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib closure_wave`

**Quality gates**: cargo check clean, cargo fmt clean, all 16 tests passing

### Files Changed
- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/closure_wave.rs` (new, ~617 lines)
- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/lib.rs` (added `pub mod closure_wave;`)

1. **Unit tests**: deterministic tests proving local semantics/invariants for the changed surface.
2. **E2E scripts**: scenario-level scripts exercising realistic flows and differential behavior where applicable.
3. **Structured logging artifacts**: machine-parseable logs with run_id, seed/config, phase markers, first-divergence indicators (when relevant), and artifact hashes.

- test identifiers + pass/fail summary,
- reproduction command(s),
- output artifact locations/hashes,
- short rationale linking evidence to acceptance criteria.

Gate policy:
- Missing any one of the three layers = bead remains open.
- Evidence must be reproducible on a clean checkout with pinned config/seeds.

 {"id":"bd-1dp9.4","title":"Track 4: Storage and Transaction Semantics Closure","description":"## Context
Parity is not only SQL output: transaction and storage semantics must match observable SQLite behavior where in scope.

## Goal
Close WAL/journal/checkpoint/transaction/file-format/recovery parity gaps.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Storage and transaction parity suites pass against oracle behavior contracts.
- Recovery edge cases produce deterministic outcomes and proof artifacts.
- Detailed logs exist for all failure paths.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:22.669594080Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:55.813624039Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mvcc","parity100","storage","transactions","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:02:22.669594080Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:03:13.517594911Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:03:13.845434210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1373,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track 4 operating notes:
- Storage/transaction parity is critical because correctness failures here are high-severity and hard to diagnose.
- Focus: WAL/checkpoint/journal semantics, lock/busy/savepoint/autocommit behavior, maintenance commands.

Design choices:
- Closure must preserve FrankenSQLite concurrency model while matching supported SQLite-visible semantics.
- Crash/torn-write handling validated via differential recovery suites and artifact proofs.

Test/logging requirements:
- Deterministic crash/recovery scenario matrix (seeded fault injection).
- E2E scripts with structured transaction timeline logs.
- Recovery logs include frame/page identifiers, recovery decision path, and final state hash checks.

Definition of done:
- Recovery and transaction behavior parity verified for declared surface with reproducible forensic artifacts.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:11:24Z"}]}

This patch did not introduce those findings.\n\n## Notes\n- SQLite oracle was manually validated () before patching: non-WAL  returns .\n- Bead remains ; this comment captures one closed parity gap plus reproducible evidence.","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:12:21Z"},{"id":1439,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Superseding prior malformed comment (shell interpolation artifact). This is the canonical evidence record for this slice.

## Scope completed
Implemented WAL/checkpoint non-WAL sentinel parity behavior for `bd-1dp9.4.1`.

### Code changes
- `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`
  - `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint` now returns SQLite-compatible sentinel `(busy=0, log=-1, checkpointed=-1)` when journal mode is not WAL.
- `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`
  - Updated unit test: `test_pragma_wal_checkpoint_returns_sentinel_in_delete_mode`.
- `crates/fsqlite-e2e/tests/correctness_transactions.rs`
  - Added Scenario K: `txn_wal_checkpoint_journal_mode_transitions_file_backed` with structured log fields (`bead_id`, `run_id`, `seed`, `phase`).

## Reproduction commands and outcomes
### Unit test evidence
- Command:
  - `cargo test -p fsqlite-core test_pragma_wal_checkpoint_returns_sentinel_in_delete_mode -- --nocapture`
- Outcome:
  - PASS (`1 passed; 0 failed`)

### E2E evidence
- Command:
  - `cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e --test correctness_transactions txn_wal_checkpoint_journal_mode_transitions_file_backed -- --nocapture`
- Outcome:
  - PASS (`1 passed; 0 failed`)
- Structured checkpoints observed:
  - `phase=mode_switch_wal c_mode=[[Text("wal")]] f_mode=[[Text("wal")]]`
  - `phase=checkpoint_passive c=(0, 4, 4) f=(0, 4, 4)`
  - `phase=mode_switch_delete c_mode=[[Text("delete")]] f_mode=[[Text("delete")]]`
  - `phase=checkpoint_nonwal c_rows=[[Integer(0), Integer(-1), Integer(-1)]] f_rows=[[Integer(0), Integer(-1), Integer(-1)]]`
  - `phase=visibility_after_nonwal_ckpt c_count=[[Integer(2)]] f_count=[[Integer(2)]]`

## Oracle rationale
Manual SQLite oracle check (`sqlite3`) confirmed non-WAL `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint` returns `0|-1|-1`; this slice aligns FrankenSQLite to that behavior.

## Gate status
- `cargo fmt --check`: PASS
- `cargo check --all-targets`: PASS (with unrelated existing warnings)
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`: FAIL due pre-existing cast-wrap lints in untouched `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs` around lines ~3344-3383.

Bead remains `in_progress`; this closes one concrete parity gap with unit + e2e + structured-logging evidence.

 {"id":"bd-mblr.7.4.3","title":"Durability drift gate and release criteria","description":"Add drift analysis and gating criteria for cross-platform durability regressions.","design":"Define policy and CI gate for durability drift that controls release readiness and escalation workflows.","acceptance_criteria":"- CI gate blocks release lanes when durability regressions exceed defined severity thresholds.
- Reports include trend data and direct links to failing matrix cell artifacts.
- E2e policy tests validate escalation and override rules under realistic failure patterns.","notes":"Override paths require explicit owner signoff and automatic follow up bead creation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:21.012202316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:08:37.546849408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.5.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:57.049969347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.7.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:21.012202316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.7.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:56.974296352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mblr.7.5","title":"Failure Forensics Navigator","description":"Build operator-centric navigation over logs, bundles, manifests, and timelines for rapid root-cause isolation without manual artifact hunting.","design":"Create a forensics navigator that correlates logs, traces, artifacts, and test metadata into a single timeline centric debugging surface.","acceptance_criteria":"- Evidence model unifies unit, integration, soak, and CI lane artifacts with stable identifiers.
- Query workflows support root cause narrowing by commit, seed, component, and failure class.
- Operator playbooks demonstrate reduced mean time to diagnosis on historical failures.","notes":"Prefer additive indexing over log rewriting so raw evidence remains immutable and auditable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:21.091141116Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:08:37.636453405Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.5","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:21.091141116Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-mblr.7.5.1","title":"Unified evidence index schema","description":"Define searchable index over run/scenario/invariant/log/artifact relationships consumed by forensics tooling.","design":"Define a normalized evidence index schema that captures provenance, timestamps, component tags, and cross artifact links.","acceptance_criteria":"- Schema supports ingestion of structured logs, benchmark outputs, replay manifests, and CI metadata.
- Validation rules reject malformed records and preserve referential integrity.
- Unit tests cover schema evolution compatibility and indexing correctness.","notes":"Design schema for append only ingestion to simplify concurrency and auditability.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:21.170714533Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:08:37.731342265Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.2.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:57.124781610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.4.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:57.201266876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.7.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:21.170714533Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-mblr.7.5.2","title":"Forensics CLI timeline and correlation queries","description":"Implement tool to query event timelines, correlate artifacts, and emit replay commands from indexed evidence.","design":"Implement CLI queries for timeline reconstruction, correlation analysis, and failure clustering on top of the evidence index.","acceptance_criteria":"- CLI supports filters by issue id, commit, seed, component, and severity with deterministic output ordering.
- Timeline view highlights key state transitions and linked artifacts for rapid inspection.
- Integration tests verify query correctness and performance on large synthetic evidence sets.","notes":"Expose machine readable output mode so CI bots can annotate failures automatically.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:21.253831123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:08:37.822821042Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.4.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:57.429286072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.4.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:57.355702590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.7.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:21.253831123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.7.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:57.276697046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mblr.7.5.3","title":"Navigator correctness tests and operator workflows","description":"Validate navigator outputs and publish operator workflows with representative failure walkthroughs.","design":"Deliver correctness tests and operator workflows that validate navigator output against known truth sets and real incidents.","acceptance_criteria":"- Golden datasets assert timeline and correlation outputs for representative incident classes.
- E2e scripts walk through end to end triage from failing lane to narrowed root cause candidate set.
- Logging standards ensure every workflow step emits structured, timestamped diagnostics.","notes":"Keep playbooks concise and scriptable so on-call usage is reliable under pressure.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:21.339440541Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:08:37.908088689Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.3.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:57.579796067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.7.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:21.339440541Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.7.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:57.504758542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mblr.7.6","title":"Automated Regression Bisect and Culprit Isolation","description":"Automate deterministic replay plus commit bisecting for recurring failures to minimize time-to-root-cause.","design":"Automate regression culprit isolation by combining deterministic replay manifests with commit range bisect orchestration.","acceptance_criteria":"- Replay manifests capture enough context to evaluate pass or fail deterministically at each candidate commit.
- Bisect orchestration minimizes reruns and reports confidence with clear culprit metadata.
- CI integration can trigger and monitor bisect jobs automatically for qualifying regressions.","notes":"Isolation workflows should prioritize developer feedback speed while preserving reproducibility guarantees.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:21.429730391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:08:38.004889760Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.6","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:21.429730391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
